title: UnderControl Skill
description: Schema for UnderControl skill entities. Skills are the unit of capability assignment — they define what a user or agent can do. Skills are group-scoped and stored in the database.

id.description: Skill UUID. Present = existing skill (update). Absent = new skill (create).

name.description: Slug-only name: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. Must start with a letter. Unique within a group. Used as the human-readable identifier (e.g., 'ud-cli', 'release-workflow').

description.description: One-liner description used for discovery and listing.

content.description: Prompt content in markdown. This is the skill's payload — the text that gets output when the skill is consumed (e.g., via 'ud prompt <name>').

metadata.description: Extensible key-value store (JSONB). Reserved for future use: parameters, tool declarations, agent config.

path.description: Virtual folder path for tree organization. Auto-set to /system/skills/{name}/ on create/update. Resources uploaded to the same path are co-located with the skill in the explorer tree.

file_path.description: Virtual file path for the skill content. Computed as /system/skills/{name}/{name}.md. Read-only, derived from the skill name.

editable.description: false = locked built-in skill (seeded on first run), true = user-created skill. Built-in skills cannot be modified or deleted.

group_id.description: Group this skill belongs to. All skills are group-scoped.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp (ISO 8601). Updated by server.

naming_convention.description: Skill names are slugs: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only. Must start with a letter. Examples: 'ud-cli', 'release-workflow', 'code-review'. Names are unique within a group — two different groups can have skills with the same name.

built_in_skills.description: Built-in skills are seeded into the database on first run with editable=false. They provide default capabilities (e.g., 'ud-cli' skill prompt). Users cannot modify or delete them, but can create their own skills with different names.

consumption_model.description: Skills are consumed via stdout: 'ud prompt <skill-name>' fetches the skill from the API and outputs its content. This is consumer-agnostic — any tool that reads stdin/stdout can use skills. Example: 'claude-code $(ud prompt my-skill)'
